== Notes for [[Item:Q111828|Q111828]] ==

Heraldic Dossier :
Duke of Estrada

Spanish crown of the Duke of Estrada nobility, This powerful family was undoubtedly formed by the connection of the house of the Duque lineage, of the Montaña de Santander, with that of the Estrada lineage, which had its primitive and very ancient site in the place of Estrada, in the municipality of Val de San Vicente and the judicial district of San Vicente de la Barquera (Santander).

On a rock there is still an old tower in that place, and on it a shield with an eagle, which constitutes the arms of the Estrada lineage.

Knights of that house of D. Estrada later settled in the town of Cangas de Onís (Oviedo), and on the occasion of marriages they later settled in the town of Llanes, in the same province.

Another house of great lustre, originally from that of Estrada, was in Talavera de la Reina (Toledo).

Another in the Peñamellera Valley, in the former Asturias of Santillana (Santander) and today belonging to the judicial district of Llanes.

In the city of Santo Domingo de la Calzada (Logroño) lived another family of this surname, originally from Santander. To it belonged José D. de Estrada, a native of Santander, who went to Santo Domingo de la Calzada, of which city he was perpetual Alderman and where he married Doña Ángela Íñiguez, a native of the aforementioned city, and they were the parents of Medel D. de Estrada and Íñiguez, a native and perpetual Regidor of Santo Domingo de la Calzada, that he married Doña Isabel de Salazar Guréndez, a native of Vega de Río Tirón, and they had Esteban D. de Estrada y Salazar as a son. ...

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
